title: The Petition of the Daughters of Zelophehad
summary: “And Zelophehad the son of Hepher had no sons, but daughters: and the names of the daughters of Zelophehad were Mahlah, and Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.” (Num 26:33). Zelophehad had five daughters who it appears were unmarried virgins. Let us call them the five wise virgins. Zelophehad means “firstborn”. Certainly, the name of their father may have influenced his daughters. Never underestimate the power of a name to shape one’s destiny. I always advise saints to choose the names of their children with prayer and wisdom. The best reminder parents can give their children, when the parents are long dead, is the name/s you choose; it will still guide and influence them from beyond the grave. “And they stood before Moses, and before Eleazar the priest, and before the princes and all the congregation, by the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, saying” (Numbers 27:2). These were clearly very brave virgins. Never before do we read of women seeking an official audience before Moses and leadership of Israel before the Tabernacle of the Lord with the entire congregation assembled. We can never hope to receive the great promises of God unless we are willing to risk our reputations and dream big dreams. “Be not afraid of their faces: for I am with thee to deliver thee, saith the LORD” (Jeremiah 1:8). Those who see the face of God have no fear of the face of a Moses, Aaron, Eleazer etc.
